perturbation.original_text: 2. For a period of 18 months, COMPANY agrees not to disclose or disseminate the Confidential Information to any third party, except for those of its employees, lawyers, accountants, insurance agents and other professional representatives who have a need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of COMPANY’S evaluation of the opportunity and who agree to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Except as necessary to evaluate the business opportunity, COMPANY shall not use, reproduce or draw upon the Confidentia1 information for any purpose or circulate it within its organization.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. COMPANY agrees not to disclose or disseminate the Confidential Information to any third party, except for those of its employees who have a need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of COMPANY’S evaluation of the opportunity and who agree to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Except as necessary to evaluate the business opportunity, COMPANY shall not use, reproduce or draw upon the Confidentia1 information for any purpose or circulate it within its organization.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'lawyers, accountants, insurance agents and other professional representatives' from the list of exceptions, we create a contradiction. Section 4 still refers to the possibility of disclosure under lawful process (potentially involving lawyers or legal representation), creating uncertainty as to whether such disclosures are permitted or constitute a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. COMPANY shall provide notice to DISCLOSING PARTY of any demand made upon it under lawful process to disclose or provide the Confidential Information. COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY if DISCLOSING PARTY elects to seek reasonable protective arrangements or oppose such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed COMPANY to disclose confidential information to a broader range of professionals (lawyers, accountants, etc.) under specific conditions. The changed text removes these professionals from the exception list. Section 4, the contradicted text, references potential disclosures under lawful process, which might involve the now-excluded professionals. Therefore, the changed text creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether disclosing information to these professionals under lawful process is now a breach of contract, creating a strong, impactful contradiction as defined in Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The confidentiality obligations of COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of the Confidential Information that: (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the public, by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of COMPANY; (ii) was known by COMPANY at the time of disclosure; (iii) was obtained by COMPANY from a third party, free of any obligation of confidence; or (iv) was independently developed by COMPANY without the use of any of the Confidential Information. Confidential Information may be disclosed by COMPANY as required by governmental regulation or pursuant to a subpoena or order of a court, agency or governmental authority which is binding upon COMPANY, provided that COMPANY shall provide prior written notice of such disclosure to DISCLOSING PARTY.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The confidentiality obligations of COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of the Confidential Information that: (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the public, by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of COMPANY; (ii) was known by COMPANY at the time of disclosure; (iii) was obtained by COMPANY from a third party, free of any obligation of confidence; or (iv) was independently developed by COMPANY without the use of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Confidential Information may be disclosed by COMPANY as required by governmental regulation or pursuant to a subpoena or order of a court, agency or governmental authority which is binding upon COMPANY, provided that COMPANY shall provide prior written notice of such disclosure to DISCLOSING PARTY.' from section 3, we create a contradiction with section 4, because section 4 refers to legal disclosure but section 3 no longer acknowledges the right of the company to disclose information in these circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. COMPANY shall provide notice to DISCLOSING PARTY of any demand made upon it under lawful process to disclose or provide the Confidential Information. COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY if DISCLOSING PARTY elects to seek reasonable protective arrangements or oppose such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed COMPANY to disclose confidential information under legal process, provided they notified DISCLOSING PARTY. This provision was removed in the changed text. Section 4 explicitly states that COMPANY shall provide notice to DISCLOSING PARTY if there is a demand to disclose confidential information under lawful process and that COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY. Since the changed text in Section 3 removes the allowance of legal disclosure, but section 4 still contains the requirements, it creates a contradiction. Section 3 no longer acknowledges COMPANY's right to disclose in these circumstances, whereas Section 4 expects that right to exist.

perturbation.original_text: 6. All Confidential Information shall remain the property of DISCLOSING PARTY. This Agreement does not grant any rights to COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information. Upon written notice from DISCLOSING PARTY at any time, COMPANY shall return to DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. All Confidential Information shall remain the property of DISCLOSING PARTY. This Agreement does not grant any rights to COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information. Upon written notice from DISCLOSING PARTY at any time, COMPANY shall return to DISCLOSING PARTY all Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof', COMPANY only needs to return all confidential information, this conflicts implicitly with Section 2, which implies that COMPANY can use the information for evaluation purposes and potentially create 'derivative works'. If COMPANY is evaluating it, then there should be derivative works. It is unclear what should happen to the existing derivative works if COMPANY is only required to return it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. COMPANY agrees not to disclose or disseminate the Confidential Information to any third party, except for those of its employees who have a need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of COMPANY’S evaluation of the opportunity and who agree to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Except as necessary to evaluate the business opportunity, COMPANY shall not use, reproduce or draw upon the Confidentia1 information for any purpose or circulate it within its organization.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the option for COMPANY to 'destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof', and now COMPANY is only required to return all confidential information. This contradicts Section 2, which states that COMPANY can use the information for evaluation purposes and potentially create 'derivative works'. Because the company is able to create derivative works under the original contract, and the new contract doesn't specify that they should be destroyed, this creates a direct conflict with the terms in Section 2, making the answer 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to Confidential Information and no modification or waiver of the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless it is in writing signed by an authorized representative of each party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement contains the agreement between the parties with respect to Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and no modification or waiver of the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless it is in writing signed by an authorized representative of each party', the Agreement becomes vague as to how it is modified. This directly impacts section 7, which states that 'Nothing contained herein shall obligate either party to proceed with any transaction', because by removing the clause, it now possible for the court to deem the Agreement to be modified without a signed document, which can change the statement present in Section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Nothing contained herein shall obligate either party to proceed with any transaction and each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate discussions relating to the business opportunity at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause specifying how modifications or waivers to the agreement should be handled (in writing, signed by authorized representatives). The changed text omits this clause. This omission creates a direct contradiction because Section 7 states that either party can terminate discussions at any time, implying no obligation to proceed. By removing the requirement for written and signed modifications, a court could potentially deem the agreement modified without such documentation, which could then obligate a party to proceed against their will, thus contradicting Section 7. The change directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of Section 7.

perturbation.original_text: 9. All Confidential Information is provided “As is” without any warranties, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy and completeness. Any representations regarding the Confidential Information, if any, will be contained in the documentation consummating the business opportunity, if any.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. All Confidential Information is provided “As is”. Any representations regarding the Confidential Information, if any, will be contained in the documentation consummating the business opportunity, if any.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without any warranties, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy and completeness.' it creates an issue because Section 1 suggests that the information includes 'information relating to the business opportunity being explored'. Without warranty disclaimers, the lack of accuracy could leave DISCLOSING party liable. The phrase 'as is' is not enough to protect if there is another clause stating the business opportunity being explored.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In connection with the business opportunity currently being explored by DISCLOSING PARTY and COMPANY, COMPANY agrees that all information obtained by it, whether in tangible or intangible form, from or about DISCLOSING PARTY, including, without limitation, information relating to the business opportunity being explored, or information relating to DISCLOSING PARTY’S products, services, technology, marketing, business plans, finances, research, development, know-how or personnel shall be considered Confidential Information. For purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information shall also include the fact that such discussions are taking place.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'without any warranties, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy and completeness' from Section 9 directly contradicts Section 1. Section 1 states that Confidential Information includes 'information relating to the business opportunity being explored'. By removing the warranty disclaimer, the disclosing party could be liable for inaccuracies in that information, conflicting with the initial intent to provide the information 'As is'. This creates a strong contradiction as it impacts liability and enforceability, where the contradicted text does not expect uncertainty.
